blocks.body: Percent means "per hundred." The symbol % represents hundredths. 45% means 45 out of 100, or 45/100 = 0.45.
Converting percent to decimal: move the decimal point two places LEFT (divide by 100). Example: 35% = 0.35, 7% = 0.07, 150% = 1.50.
Converting decimal to percent: move the decimal point two places RIGHT (multiply by 100). Example: 0.6 = 60%, 0.025 = 2.5%, 1.3 = 130%.
Converting percent to fraction: write the percent over 100 and simplify. Example: 25% = 25/100 = 1/4. 60% = 60/100 = 3/5.

blocks.text: Finding a Percent of a Number

blocks.body: To find a percent of a number: convert the percent to a decimal, then multiply. Formula: Part = Percent x Whole. Example: 30% of 80 = 0.30 x 80 = 24.
The three percent problems: (1) Find the part: Part = % x Whole. (2) Find the percent: % = Part / Whole. (3) Find the whole: Whole = Part / %.
